{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }}
'''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters.
== Overview ==
Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers.
The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.

The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": - '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. - '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. - '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.

The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": - '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. - '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. - '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }}
'''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters.
== Overview ==
Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers.
The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.

The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": - '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. - '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. - '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986.

The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": - '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. - '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. - '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.

Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": - '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. - '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. - '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }}
'''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters.
== Overview ==
Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers.
The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>

The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": - '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. - '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. - '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }}
'''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters.
== Overview ==
Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers.
The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": - '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. - '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. - '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Ise-Shima National Park|ja|伊勢志摩国立公園}} * {{ill|Navigation|ja|航路}} * {{ill|Landmarks|ja|航路標識}} * {{ill|Hiking|ja|ハイキング}} * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)}}

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }}
'''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters.
== Overview ==
Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers.
The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": - '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. - '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. - '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. - '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986.

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }}
'''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters.
== Overview ==
Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers.
The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Ise-Shima National Park|ja|伊勢志摩国立公園}} * {{ill|Navigation|ja|航路}} * {{ill|Landmarks|ja|航路標識}} * {{ill|Hiking|ja|ハイキング}} * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)}}
== External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }}
'''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters.
== Overview ==
Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers.
The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986.

The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Ise-Shima National Park|ja|伊勢志摩国立公園}} * {{ill|Navigation|ja|航路}} * {{ill|Landmarks|ja|航路標識}} * {{ill|Hiking|ja|ハイキング}} * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)}}
== External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }}
'''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters.
== Overview ==
Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers.
The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局}}.
The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref>
== Geography and Geology ==
Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref>
== Aonomine Trails ==
The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Ise-Shima National Park|ja|伊勢志摩国立公園}} * {{ill|Navigation|ja|航路}} * {{ill|Landmarks|ja|航路標識}} * {{ill|Hiking|ja|ハイキング}} * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)}}
== External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]

The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref> == Geography and Geology == Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref> == Aonomine Trails == The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅}} in Shima City. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ise-Shima National Park|ja|伊勢志摩国立公園}} * {{ill|Navigation|ja|航路}} * {{ill|Landmarks|ja|航路標識}} * {{ill|Hiking|ja|ハイキング}} * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)}} == External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]

The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers. The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ|12=draft|12=draft}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道|12=draft|12=draft}}. [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page] <!--Aohou Mountain-->

{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }} '''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between {{ill|Toba City|ja|鳥羽市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It has an elevation of 336 meters. == Overview == Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of {{ill|Ise Bay|ja|伊勢湾|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Enshū-nada Sea|ja|遠州灘|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kumano-nada Sea|ja|熊野灘|12=draft|12=draft}}, making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船|12=draft|12=draft}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=draft|12=draft}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨|12=draft|12=draft}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers. The mountain’s forests of {{ill|Castanopsis|ja|シイ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ|12=draft|12=draft}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道|12=draft|12=draft}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林|12=draft|12=draft}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局|12=draft|12=draft}}. The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰|12=draft|12=draft}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en|12=draft|12=draft}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議|12=draft|12=draft}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref> == Geography and Geology == Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川|12=draft|12=draft}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群|12=draft|12=draft}}, formed during the {{ill|Mesozoic Era|ja|中生代|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref> == Aonomine Trails == The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near {{ill|Gochi Station|ja|五知駅|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shima City. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ise-Shima National Park|ja|伊勢志摩国立公園|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Navigation|ja|航路|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Landmarks|ja|航路標識|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hiking|ja|ハイキング|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page] <!--Aohou Mountain-->

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:青峰山 up to rev 92556814
revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 青峰山 |画像 = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション = 志摩市側より |標高 = 336.3 | 緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =24 |緯度秒 =32 | 経度度 =136 |経度分 =49 |経度秒 =27 |所在地 = {{JPN}}[[三重県]][[鳥羽市]]・[[志摩市]] |山系 = 独立峰 |種類 = 孤峰低山性山地<ref>鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：上巻23ページ</ref> |初登頂 = }}
'''青峰山'''（あおのみねさん<ref>三重県（2005）"[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf 加茂川水系河川整備基本方針]"3ページ</ref>）は[[三重県]][[鳥羽市]]と[[志摩市]]の市境に位置する山。標高336m。
== 概要 == 標高は低いものの[[伊勢湾]]、[[遠州灘]]、[[熊野灘]]から見通せるため、古くから沖合いを行く[[廻船]]の船乗りたちから目印とされてきた。このため、山頂付近にある[[正福寺 (鳥羽市)|正福寺]]は海の安全を守る[[仏]]様（[[鯨]]の背に乗った[[金|黄金]]の[[観音]]様）を安置し、古くから船の安全祈願を行う寺とされてきた。山には[[シイ]]や[[カシ]]が繁っており良好な[[景観]]を形成していることから、[[近畿自然歩道]]が設定されている。また、森林自体も[[グローバル・ポジショニング・システム]]の発達により存在価値は低下したものの、[[保安林|航行目標保安林]]として保護されている。山頂付近には展望台のほか、デジタルテレビ放送の中継施設（[[磯部中継局]]）が存在している。
山麓の[[港町]]・[[磯部町的矢|的矢]]出身の[[嶋田青峰]]の俳号はこの山に由来する<ref>細井（1996）：48ページ</ref>。[[2016年]]（平成28年）[[5月25日]]、[[第42回先進国首脳会議]]（伊勢志摩サミット）を前に来日中だった[[カナダ]]の[[カナダ首相|首相]]・[[ジャスティン・トルドー]]が妻の{{仮リンク|ソフィー・グレゴワール・トルドー|en|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau}}とともに青峰山を登り、正福寺境内を散策するなどして[[結婚記念日]]に合わせた休暇を楽しんだ<ref>「カナダ首相は夫婦で山登り 結婚記念日を満喫」[[中日新聞]]2016年5月26日付朝刊</ref>。
== 地形・地質 == 青峰山は鳥羽市を島嶼列地区・[[朝熊山|朝熊ヶ岳]]地区・東部台地区・加茂川沖積地区・海食台地区の5つに分類した時、100〜115m級の[[丘陵]]地帯である「東部台地区」に分類され、その中では最も高い<ref>鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：上巻18ページ</ref>。鳥羽市内を流れる[[二級河川]]加茂川の[[源流]]地になっている<ref>鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：上巻20ページ</ref>。
[[地質学]]的には的矢層群に分類される<ref>鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：上巻19ページ</ref>。的矢層群は[[四万十層群|四万十帯]]の一種であり、[[中生代]]の[[地層]]である<ref>河合（1967）：44ページ</ref>。 == 青峰道 == 青峰山の登山道は「青峰道」と総称される。鳥羽市側からの登山道は、松尾道と堅子道の2ルート、志摩市側からの登山道は、的矢道・磯部道・五知道の3ルートある。[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]]が作成し、[[鉄道駅|駅]]などで配布している「てくてくまっぷ 青峰山正福寺コース」では[[近鉄志摩線]][[松尾駅 (三重県)|松尾駅]]を起点とし、松尾道（旧道）を経て青峰山正福寺に至り、磯部道を通って同線[[沓掛駅]]を終点とするコースが紹介されている。[[自動車]]利用の場合は、お船祭りの際に通行規制がかかり、鳥羽市側の松尾道（新道）を利用して登り、志摩市側の的矢道を利用して下る[[一方通行]]が実施される<ref name="ithu23">磯部町史編纂委員会 編（1997）：823ページ</ref>。
;松尾道<ref>三重県教育委員会 編（1986）：213〜224ページ</ref> :鳥羽市側からの登山道。[[1962年]]（昭和37年）に開通した自動車で登ることのできる新道と、以前からの徒歩で登る旧道の2つがある。鳥羽市松尾町の松尾交差点（[[国道167号]]交点）を起点とし、松尾集落を抜けて山頂に向かう。26丁ある<ref name="thg">鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：下巻705ページ</ref>。
;堅子道<ref name="ithu23"/> :鳥羽市側を起点とする登山道。鳥羽市堅子町を起点とし、志摩市磯部町山田の奥部地区で的矢道と合流する。
;磯部道<ref>三重県教育委員会 編（1986）：203〜209ページ</ref> :志摩方面からの主要な登山道である。志摩市[[磯部町上之郷]]の[[伊雑宮]]を起点に磯部町山田、磯部町沓掛を経て山頂に至る。登山口から山頂まで17丁ある。自動車利用不可。
;的矢道<ref>三重県教育委員会 編（1986）：200〜202ページ</ref> :志摩市側からの登山道。志摩市[[磯部町的矢]]の的矢村神社の北側を起点とし、幣振坂、磯部町山田の奥部を経由して山頂に至る。奥部から山頂までの区間は舗装され、自動車利用が可能である。一部に残されている徒歩専用の旧道は通行困難な区間が存在する。66丁ある<ref name="thg"/>。
;五知道<ref>三重県教育委員会 編（1986）：210〜212ページ</ref> :志摩市側からの登山道。志摩市[[磯部町五知]]の下五知集落入り口（[[五知駅]]前）を起点とし、東方向に進み山頂に至る。主に磯部町五知の住民に利用され、その他の[[地域]]の住民が利用することは少ない。自動車利用不可。丁石はない<ref name="thg"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 磯部町史編纂委員会 編『磯部町史 上巻』磯部町、平成9年9月1日、1356pp. * 河合正虎（1967）"[http://www.gsj.jp/Pub/News/pdf/1967/04/67_04_03.pdf 日本列島の生い立ちを探る④-2]"地質ニュース（地質調査所）.4:42-56. * 鳥羽市史編さん室 編『鳥羽市史 上巻』鳥羽市役所、平成3年3月25日、1126pp. * 鳥羽市史編さん室 編『鳥羽市史 下巻』鳥羽市役所、平成3年3月25日、1347pp. * 細井啓司（1996）"『ホトトギス』と嶋田青峰"俳句文学館紀要（[[社団法人]]俳人協会）.9:47-66. * 三重県教育委員会 編『伊勢街道 朝熊岳道・二見道・磯部道・青峰道・鳥羽道 ―歴史の道調査報告書―』三重県教育委員会、1986年3月31日、281pp. * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ 青峰山正福寺（チェック三重ホームページ）]
== 関連項目 == * [[伊勢志摩国立公園]] * [[航路]] * [[航路標識]] * [[ハイキング]] * [[日和山 (鳥羽市)]] - 青峰山同様、航行の目標とされた山 * [[正福寺 (鳥羽市)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kirari1000.com/www.kirari1000.com.original.03656.base_data..html 青峰山] - [[伊勢志摩きらり千選]]（財団法人伊勢志摩国立公園協会）

revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 青峰山 |画像 = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション = 志摩市側より |標高 = 336.3 | 緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =24 |緯度秒 =32 | 経度度 =136 |経度分 =49 |経度秒 =27 |所在地 = {{JPN}}[[三重県]][[鳥羽市]]・[[志摩市]] |山系 = 独立峰 |種類 = 孤峰低山性山地<ref>鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：上巻23ページ</ref> |初登頂 = }}
'''青峰山'''（あおのみねさん<ref>三重県（2005）"[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf 加茂川水系河川整備基本方針]"3ページ</ref>）は[[三重県]][[鳥羽市]]と[[志摩市]]の市境に位置する山。標高336m。
== 概要 == 標高は低いものの[[伊勢湾]]、[[遠州灘]]、[[熊野灘]]から見通せるため、古くから沖合いを行く[[廻船]]の船乗りたちから目印とされてきた。このため、山頂付近にある[[正福寺 (鳥羽市)|正福寺]]は海の安全を守る[[仏]]様（[[鯨]]の背に乗った[[金|黄金]]の[[観音]]様）を安置し、古くから船の安全祈願を行う寺とされてきた。山には[[シイ]]や[[カシ]]が繁っており良好な[[景観]]を形成していることから、[[近畿自然歩道]]が設定されている。また、森林自体も[[グローバル・ポジショニング・システム]]の発達により存在価値は低下したものの、[[保安林|航行目標保安林]]として保護されている。山頂付近には展望台のほか、デジタルテレビ放送の中継施設（[[磯部中継局]]）が存在している。
山麓の[[港町]]・[[磯部町的矢|的矢]]出身の[[嶋田青峰]]の俳号はこの山に由来する<ref>細井（1996）：48ページ</ref>。[[2016年]]（平成28年）[[5月25日]]、[[第42回先進国首脳会議]]（伊勢志摩サミット）を前に来日中だった[[カナダ]]の[[カナダ首相|首相]]・[[ジャスティン・トルドー]]が妻の{{仮リンク|ソフィー・グレゴワール・トルドー|en|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau}}とともに青峰山を登り、正福寺境内を散策するなどして[[結婚記念日]]に合わせた休暇を楽しんだ<ref>「カナダ首相は夫婦で山登り 結婚記念日を満喫」[[中日新聞]]2016年5月26日付朝刊</ref>。
== 地形・地質 == 青峰山は鳥羽市を島嶼列地区・[[朝熊山|朝熊ヶ岳]]地区・東部台地区・加茂川沖積地区・海食台地区の5つに分類した時、100〜115m級の[[丘陵]]地帯である「東部台地区」に分類され、その中では最も高い<ref>鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：上巻18ページ</ref>。鳥羽市内を流れる[[二級河川]]加茂川の[[源流]]地になっている<ref>鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：上巻20ページ</ref>。
[[地質学]]的には的矢層群に分類される<ref>鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：上巻19ページ</ref>。的矢層群は[[四万十層群|四万十帯]]の一種であり、[[中生代]]の[[地層]]である<ref>河合（1967）：44ページ</ref>。 == 青峰道 == 青峰山の登山道は「青峰道」と総称される。鳥羽市側からの登山道は、松尾道と堅子道の2ルート、志摩市側からの登山道は、的矢道・磯部道・五知道の3ルートある。[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]]が作成し、[[鉄道駅|駅]]などで配布している「てくてくまっぷ 青峰山正福寺コース」では[[近鉄志摩線]][[松尾駅 (三重県)|松尾駅]]を起点とし、松尾道（旧道）を経て青峰山正福寺に至り、磯部道を通って同線[[沓掛駅]]を終点とするコースが紹介されている。[[自動車]]利用の場合は、お船祭りの際に通行規制がかかり、鳥羽市側の松尾道（新道）を利用して登り、志摩市側の的矢道を利用して下る[[一方通行]]が実施される<ref name="ithu23">磯部町史編纂委員会 編（1997）：823ページ</ref>。
;松尾道<ref>三重県教育委員会 編（1986）：213〜224ページ</ref> :鳥羽市側からの登山道。[[1962年]]（昭和37年）に開通した自動車で登ることのできる新道と、以前からの徒歩で登る旧道の2つがある。鳥羽市松尾町の松尾交差点（[[国道167号]]交点）を起点とし、松尾集落を抜けて山頂に向かう。26丁ある<ref name="thg">鳥羽市史編さん室 編（1991）：下巻705ページ</ref>。
;堅子道<ref name="ithu23"/> :鳥羽市側を起点とする登山道。鳥羽市堅子町を起点とし、志摩市磯部町山田の奥部地区で的矢道と合流する。
;磯部道<ref>三重県教育委員会 編（1986）：203〜209ページ</ref> :志摩方面からの主要な登山道である。志摩市[[磯部町上之郷]]の[[伊雑宮]]を起点に磯部町山田、磯部町沓掛を経て山頂に至る。登山口から山頂まで17丁ある。自動車利用不可。
;的矢道<ref>三重県教育委員会 編（1986）：200〜202ページ</ref> :志摩市側からの登山道。志摩市[[磯部町的矢]]の的矢村神社の北側を起点とし、幣振坂、磯部町山田の奥部を経由して山頂に至る。奥部から山頂までの区間は舗装され、自動車利用が可能である。一部に残されている徒歩専用の旧道は通行困難な区間が存在する。66丁ある<ref name="thg"/>。
;五知道<ref>三重県教育委員会 編（1986）：210〜212ページ</ref> :志摩市側からの登山道。志摩市[[磯部町五知]]の下五知集落入り口（[[五知駅]]前）を起点とし、東方向に進み山頂に至る。主に磯部町五知の住民に利用され、その他の[[地域]]の住民が利用することは少ない。自動車利用不可。丁石はない<ref name="thg"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 磯部町史編纂委員会 編『磯部町史 上巻』磯部町、平成9年9月1日、1356pp. * 河合正虎（1967）"[http://www.gsj.jp/Pub/News/pdf/1967/04/67_04_03.pdf 日本列島の生い立ちを探る④-2]"地質ニュース（地質調査所）.4:42-56. * 鳥羽市史編さん室 編『鳥羽市史 上巻』鳥羽市役所、平成3年3月25日、1126pp. * 鳥羽市史編さん室 編『鳥羽市史 下巻』鳥羽市役所、平成3年3月25日、1347pp. * 細井啓司（1996）"『ホトトギス』と嶋田青峰"俳句文学館紀要（[[社団法人]]俳人協会）.9:47-66. * 三重県教育委員会 編『伊勢街道 朝熊岳道・二見道・磯部道・青峰道・鳥羽道 ―歴史の道調査報告書―』三重県教育委員会、1986年3月31日、281pp. * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ 青峰山正福寺（チェック三重ホームページ）]
== 関連項目 == * [[伊勢志摩国立公園]] * [[航路]] * [[航路標識]] * [[ハイキング]] * [[日和山 (鳥羽市)]] - 青峰山同様、航行の目標とされた山 * [[正福寺 (鳥羽市)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kirari1000.com/www.kirari1000.com.original.03656.base_data..html 青峰山] - [[伊勢志摩きらり千選]]（財団法人伊勢志摩国立公園協会）

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title|qq=}} | location = {{JPN|qq=}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }} '''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between [[:en:Toba, Mie|Toba City]] and [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima City]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It has an elevation of 336 meters. == Overview == Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]], [[:en:Enshū Sea|Enshū-nada Sea]], and [[:en:Kumano Sea|Kumano-nada Sea]], making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kaisen|qq=}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofuku-ji Temple|qq=}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/whale|qq=}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers. The mountain’s forests of [[:en:Castanopsis|Castanopsis]] and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki Nature Trail|qq=}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protective Forest for Navigation|qq=}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Relay Station|qq=}}. The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihō Shimada|qq=}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Justin Trudeau|qq=}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|qq=}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/42nd G7 Summit|qq=}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref> == Geography and Geology == Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|qq=}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoya Formation|qq=}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanto Belt|qq=}}, formed during the [[:en:Mesozoic|Mesozoic Era]].<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref> == Aonomine Trails == The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near [[:en:Gochi Station|Gochi Station]] in Shima City. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ise-Shima National Park|Ise-Shima National Park]] * [[:en:Sea lane|Navigation]] * [[:en:Sea mark|Landmarks]] * [[:en:Hiking|Hiking]] * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]
{{translated page|ja|青峰山|version=92556814|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]] }}

Trudeau|qq=}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|qq=}} hiked Mount Aonomine during revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title|qq=}} | location = {{JPN|qq=}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }} '''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between [[:en:Toba, Mie|Toba City]] and [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima City]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It has an elevation of 336 meters. == Overview == Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]], [[:en:Enshū Sea|Enshū-nada Sea]], and [[:en:Kumano Sea|Kumano-nada Sea]], making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kaisen|qq=}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofuku-ji Temple|qq=}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/whale|qq=}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers. The mountain’s forests of [[:en:Castanopsis|Castanopsis]] and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki Nature Trail|qq=}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protective Forest for Navigation|qq=}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Relay Station|qq=}}. The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihō Shimada|qq=}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Justin Trudeau|qq=}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|qq=}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/42nd G7 Summit|qq=}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref> == Geography and Geology == Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|qq=}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoya Formation|qq=}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanto Belt|qq=}}, formed during the [[:en:Mesozoic|Mesozoic Era]].<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref> == Aonomine Trails == The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near [[:en:Gochi Station|Gochi Station]] in Shima City. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ise-Shima National Park|Ise-Shima National Park]] * [[:en:Sea lane|Navigation]] * [[:en:Sea mark|Landmarks]] * [[:en:Hiking|Hiking]] * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]
{{translated page|ja|青峰山|version=92556814|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Trudeau|qq=}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|qq=}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/42nd G7 Summit|qq=}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref> == Geography and Geology == Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|qq=}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoya Formation|qq=}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanto Belt|qq=}}, formed during the [[:en:Mesozoic|Mesozoic Era]].<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref> == Aonomine Trails == The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near [[:en:Gochi Station|Gochi Station]] in Shima City. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ise-Shima National Park|Ise-Shima National Park]] * [[:en:Sea lane|Navigation]] * [[:en:Sea mark|Landmarks]] * [[:en:Hiking|Hiking]] * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Geography of Shima, Mie
revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title|qq=}} | location = {{JPN|qq=}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }} '''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between [[:en:Toba, Mie|Toba City]] and [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima City]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It has an elevation of 336 meters. == Overview == Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]], [[:en:Enshū Sea|Enshū-nada Sea]], and [[:en:Kumano Sea|Kumano-nada Sea]], making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kaisen|qq=}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofuku-ji Temple|qq=}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/whale|qq=}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers. The mountain’s forests of [[:en:Castanopsis|Castanopsis]] and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki Nature Trail|qq=}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protective Forest for Navigation|qq=}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Relay Station|qq=}}. The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihō Shimada|qq=}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Justin Trudeau|qq=}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|qq=}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/42nd G7 Summit|qq=}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref> == Geography and Geology == Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|qq=}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoya Formation|qq=}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanto Belt|qq=}}, formed during the [[:en:Mesozoic|Mesozoic Era]].<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref> == Aonomine Trails == The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near [[:en:Gochi Station|Gochi Station]] in Shima City. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ise-Shima National Park|Ise-Shima National Park]] * [[:en:Sea lane|Navigation]] * [[:en:Sea mark|Landmarks]] * [[:en:Hiking|Hiking]] * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Aonomine | image = [[File:Mt. Aonomine from Shimonogo, Isobe.jpg|250px]] | caption = View from the Shima City side | elevation_m = 336.3 | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|32|N|136|49|27|E|type:mountain|display=inline,title|qq=}} | location = {{JPN|qq=}} [[Mie Prefecture]] ([[Toba City]] and [[Shima City]]) | range = Isolated mountain | type = Low-altitude, solitary mountain<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.23</ref> }} '''Mount Aonomine''' (青峰山, Aonomine-san<ref>Mie Prefecture (2005) "[http://www.pref.mie.jp/KAWASABO/HP/kasen/seibi/kamokihon.pdf Basic Policy for River Improvement in the Kamo River System]" p.3</ref>) is a mountain located on the border between [[:en:Toba, Mie|Toba City]] and [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima City]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It has an elevation of 336 meters. == Overview == Despite its relatively low elevation, Mount Aonomine offers clear views of [[:en:Ise Bay|Ise Bay]], [[:en:Enshū Sea|Enshū-nada Sea]], and [[:en:Kumano Sea|Kumano-nada Sea]], making it a landmark for {{ill|kaisen|ja|廻船|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kaisen|qq=}} sailors in ancient times. The mountain is home to {{ill|Shofuku-ji Temple|ja|正福寺 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofuku-ji Temple|qq=}}, which enshrines a golden statue of {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} riding on a {{ill|whale|ja|鯨|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/whale|qq=}}. The temple has historically been a site for maritime safety prayers. The mountain’s forests of [[:en:Castanopsis|Castanopsis]] and {{ill|Quercus|ja|カシ|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus|qq=}} trees contribute to its scenic beauty, making it a popular spot on the {{ill|Kinki Nature Trail|ja|近畿自然歩道|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki Nature Trail|qq=}}. While the advent of GPS has reduced its significance as a navigational aid, the area is preserved as a {{ill|Protective Forest for Navigation|ja|航行目標保安林|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protective Forest for Navigation|qq=}}. The summit features an observation deck and a digital television relay station, the {{ill|Isobe Relay Station|ja|磯部中継局|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Relay Station|qq=}}. The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihō Shimada|qq=}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Justin Trudeau|qq=}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|qq=}} hiked Mount Aonomine during their visit for the {{ill|42nd G7 Summit|en|第42回先進国首脳会議|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/42nd G7 Summit|qq=}} in Ise-Shima.<ref>"Canadian Prime Minister Enjoys Hiking with His Wife on Their Wedding Anniversary," *Chunichi Shimbun*, May 26, 2016</ref> == Geography and Geology == Mount Aonomine is classified as part of the "Eastern Terrace District," a 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|ja|加茂川|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo River (Mie Prefecture)|qq=}}, a secondary river.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.20</ref> Geologically, it belongs to the {{ill|Matoya Formation|ja|的矢層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoya Formation|qq=}}, which is part of the {{ill|Shimanto Belt|ja|四万十層群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimanto Belt|qq=}}, formed during the [[:en:Mesozoic|Mesozoic Era]].<ref>Kawai (1967): p.44</ref> == Aonomine Trails == The mountain has several trails collectively known as "Aonomine Trails": * '''Matsuo Trail''': Two routes (old and new) starting from Matsuo in Toba City. * '''Kenko Trail''': A route starting in Kenko in Toba City. * '''Isobe Trail''': Starting from Izawa-no-Miya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Matoya Trail''': A drivable route from Matoya Shrine in Shima City. * '''Gochi Trail''': A pedestrian-only route starting near [[:en:Gochi Station|Gochi Station]] in Shima City. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Isobe Town History Compilation Committee, *Isobe Town History: Upper Volume*, 1997. * Kawai Masatora, "Exploring the Origins of the Japanese Archipelago," *Geological News*, 1967. * Toba City History Compilation Office, *Toba City History: Upper and Lower Volumes*, 1991. * Hosoi Keiji, "Seihō Shimada and the Haiku Literary Movement," *Haiku Literature Quarterly*, 1996. * Mie Prefecture Board of Education, *Historical Pathways of Ise: Asama-Toba Road and Others*, 1986. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Ise-Shima National Park|Ise-Shima National Park]] * [[:en:Sea lane|Navigation]] * [[:en:Sea mark|Landmarks]] * [[:en:Hiking|Hiking]] * {{ill|Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|ja|日和山 (鳥羽市)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoriyama (Toba City)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://maikoh.net/corp/event/aominezan/ Shofuku-ji Temple - Aonomine Mountain Official Page]
{{translated page|ja|青峰山|version=92556814|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The haiku pen name of {{ill|Seihō Shimada|ja|嶋田青峰|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihō Shimada|qq=}}, a poet from nearby Matoya, is derived from this mountain.<ref>Hosoi (1996): p.48</ref> On May 25, 2016, Canadian Prime Minister {{ill|Justin Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Justin Trudeau|qq=}} and his wife {{ill|Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|en|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sophie Grégoire Trudeau|qq=}} hiked Mount Aonomine during 100–115 meter hilly region within Toba City.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): Upper Volume, p.18</ref> It serves as the source of the {{ill|Kamo River (Mie
